title: Two-Level Predictive-Coding Pass

runtime.expected_outcome: Sweep of PredictiveCodingPass converges mu1 toward the observation and mu2 toward mu1; free energy drops smoothly.

audiences.kid: The top of your brain guesses, the bottom checks, and they nudge each other until they agree.
audiences.real: Top-down predictions meet bottom-up errors. One sweep of PredictiveCodingPass applies the gradient step; repeat until F stabilises.
audiences.equation: mu_1 update blends bottom-up error (pi_1 * (y-mu_1)) with top-down error (-pi_2 * (mu_1-mu_2)). mu_2 update pulls toward mu_1.
audiences.derivation: Gradient descent on Gaussian free energy F = 0.5 * (pi_1 (y-mu_1)^2 + pi_2 (mu_1-mu_2)^2). Each belief update is a step on its local gradient (Rao & Ballard 1999; Bogacz 2017).

orchestrate.objective: Execute a full 2-level predictive-coding sweep against a fixed observation.
orchestrate.context: One observation y=1.0; mu1 = mu2 = 0; sweep 100 iterations.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 5.
Rao & Ballard (1999), Predictive coding in the visual cortex.
